confirmed.note: getFilesForDeletion vs getFilesForDownload — same iterate-m_fileMarks-and-collect-by-markType skeleton copied, only the FileMarkType enum and the score-sort tail edited; classic copy-then-tweak of sibling accessors in a freshly-added file

autoMarkFilesForDeletion vs autoMarkFilesForDownload share the db-open guard + per-row QSqlQuery loop + std::sort(lambda) + mark/emit skeleton; bodies diverge in the SQL and threshold logic but the structural block was copied and partially edited

false_positives.what_happened: Five sibling setters (setNormalEpisodes/setTotalNormalEpisodes/setNormalViewed/setOtherEpisodes/setOtherViewed) are each a single-line `m_x = std::max(0, count);`. Token-stream is near-identical across the five because trivial accessors carry almost no distinguishing tokens — only the member-name differs. This is generated-shaped getter/setter boilerplate from a struct->class SOLID conversion, not plagiarised logic.
false_positives.fix_proposal: #056: add a trivial-accessor filter — drop candidate blocks whose body is <=2 statements AND consists only of a single assignment/return touching one member (regex `m_\w+\s*=` or `return m_\w+`). Equivalently raise min-distinct-token / min-statement floor so 1-3 line setter/getter bodies never become chunk candidates.

false_positives.what_happened: loadSettings and saveSettings each contain 4 near-identical 3-line blocks of the Qt settings idiom (`q.prepare("SELECT value FROM settings WHERE name='...'"); if (q.exec() && q.next()) {...}` / `INSERT OR REPLACE ...`). The repetition is a key/value data table expressed as inline code; only the setting name string and target member differ. Flagging it as a copied chunk is misleading — it is structurally a config table, not duplicated business logic.
false_positives.fix_proposal: #056: treat blocks that differ only in a string literal + one member/lvalue as a 'data-table row' and collapse them: when N>=3 sibling blocks in one function are token-identical except for a quoted literal, emit at most one low-severity 'repeated literal-parameterised block' signal instead of N pairwise copy hits. Add string-literal masking before block hashing so literal-only variation does not register as a near-miss copy.

false_positives.what_happened: Every *Manager class added in this commit opens with the identical Qt skeleton: `Ctor(QObject*parent):QObject(parent){}` + `ensureTablesExist(){ db=QSqlDatabase::database(); if(!db.isOpen()){LOG(...);return;} QSqlQuery q(db); q.exec("CREATE TABLE ..."); }`. The ctor and the db-open guard are byte-identical across DeletionHistoryManager/DeletionLockManager/WatchSessionManager; only the CREATE TABLE schema differs. Cross-file detector sees the shared header/guard as a copied chunk.
false_positives.fix_proposal: archcheck/#056: maintain a small framework-idiom denylist (Qt: empty-body QObject ctor `: QObject(parent) {}`, the `database();/isOpen() guard`, moc-generated bodies) and strip those leading idiom lines before computing block similarity, so only the differing schema/body drives the match. Combined with the trivial-accessor floor this removes the bulk of the cross-file manager-boilerplate FPs.

cycle.desc: no include cycle — archcheck --graph reports sccs_cyclic=0, largest_scc=1 over 194 nodes / 225 edges
cycle.evidence: Single authored module tree usagi/src/ (55 .cpp); no vendored/third_party/imgui dirs present. Include DAG is acyclic; nothing to attribute.
